name: Corn chip (food medium)
basis: per 100 g as consumed (fried, salted corn chip; ~1% moisture)

components.name: starch
components.evidence: Gelatinized cornmeal starch. DOMINANT carbon/energy source - a microbe grows on corn chips chiefly by amylolysis, not on free sugars or organic acids.

components.name: total dietary fiber
components.evidence: Corn cell-wall arabinoxylan/bran; slowly fermentable.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Only measurable free sugar; equals total sugars (0.27 g).

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Measured 0 g free glucose in the finished chip (starch is polymerized).

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Measured 0 g.

components.name: phytic acid (myo-inositol hexakisphosphate)
components.source: Raw maize ~1 g/100g minus ~25-35% nixtamalization loss (Urizar & Bressani 1997, reviewed Int J Food Sci 2025 doi:10.1155/ijfo/6961009)
components.evidence: Largest organic-acid pool of a corn chip and the principal bound-phosphorus form / mineral chelator. Estimate (range 0.6-0.75 g/100g), not a direct assay of this product; lime nixtamalization is evidenced by the elevated calcium.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: Most abundant free organic acid in maize grain, 16.7 mg/100g DW; ~10 mg/100g in chip after ~60% corn-solid scaling. Small but genuine.

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Maize grain 4.91 mg/100g DW; ~3 mg/100g in chip.

components.name: acetic acid
components.evidence: Maize grain 3.81 mg/100g DW; ~2.3 mg/100g.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Maize grain 2.20 mg/100g DW; ~1.3 mg/100g.

components.name: formic acid
components.evidence: Maize grain 2.31 mg/100g DW; ~1.4 mg/100g.

components.name: lactic acid
components.evidence: Maize grain 1.40 mg/100g DW; ~0.8 mg/100g. Not a fermented food, so lactate is trace.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Maize zein/glutelin protein; glutamate-, leucine-, proline-rich; lysine- and tryptophan-limiting.

components.name: L-glutamate (Glx)
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid of maize protein.

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: High-Leu zein signature.

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: Abundant in maize prolamins.

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: Absent from current FooDB record; added.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: Absent from current FooDB record; added.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: L-serine (current FooDB record listed non-biological D-serine).

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: First-limiting amino acid of maize (low).

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: Reported as cystine (2x cysteine).

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Second-limiting amino acid of maize.

components.name: total lipid (fat)
components.evidence: Corn/vegetable frying oil.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid, from corn oil.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1)
components.evidence: MUFA 18:1.

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: Main saturated fatty acid.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Minor n-3 PUFA.

components.name: arachidic acid (20:0)
components.evidence: Long-chain saturate.

components.name: lignoceric acid (24:0)
components.evidence: Trace long-chain saturate.

components.name: behenic acid (22:0)
components.evidence: Trace long-chain saturate.

components.name: myristic acid (14:0)
components.evidence: Trace.

components.name: Sodium
components.evidence: Added table salt (NaCl); paired with chloride.

components.name: Chloride
components.source: derived from added NaCl (Na 514 mg x 1.54)
components.evidence: Counter-ion of the added salt; not separately assayed by USDA.

components.name: Phosphorus
components.evidence: Total P; much bound as phytate (see phytic acid).

components.name: Calcium
components.evidence: Elevated by lime (Ca(OH)2) nixtamalization of the corn masa.

components.name: Iron
components.evidence: Total Fe (covers the FooDB Fe2+/Fe3+ entries).

components.name: Fluoride
components.evidence: From process water; trace.

components.name: Molybdenum
components.source: genuine ultratrace nutrient of cereals; not quantified by USDA
components.evidence: Retained as a trace essential; amount not measured for this food.

components.name: Sulfate
components.evidence: Minor; retained as an ionic sulfur source.

components.name: Ammonium
components.evidence: Retained as an inorganic N ion; not separately assayed.

components.name: Vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: High, from corn oil (corrects the unquantified FooDB entry).

components.name: gamma-tocopherol
components.evidence: Actually the dominant tocopherol vitamer in corn oil; larger than alpha.

components.name: Niacin (B3)
components.evidence: Nicotinate; nixtamalization liberates bound niacin.

components.name: Folate (B9)
components.evidence: All food folate; no folic-acid fortification.

components.name: Glycine betaine
components.evidence: Osmoprotectant; measured but trace in this product.

components.name: Vitamin K1 (phylloquinone)
components.evidence: From the frying oil (phytomenadione).

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Yellow-corn provitamin A.

components.name: beta-cryptoxanthin
components.evidence: Provitamin-A xanthophyll of yellow corn.

components.name: alpha-carotene
components.evidence: Trace provitamin A.

components.name: Vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: Entirely from provitamin-A carotenoids (preformed retinol = 0).

components.name: Vitamin C (ascorbate)
components.evidence: Not detected in the finished chip; retained at null level.

components.name: Vitamin B12
components.evidence: Zero in a plant food; the FooDB 'Cyanocobalamin' entry has no real content.

components.name: Vitamin D
components.evidence: Zero; the FooDB 'Vitamin D' entry has no real content.

components.name: Water
components.evidence: Very low moisture (fried crisp).

components.name: Ash
components.evidence: Total mineral ash (includes added salt).

remove.name: Cholesterol
remove.reason: Zero in a plant + vegetable-oil food (USDA measured 0 mg); FooDB animal-sterol artifact.

remove.name: Lactose C12H22O11
remove.reason: No dairy in a corn/oil/salt product; USDA measured 0 g. Implausible.

remove.name: D-Galactose
remove.reason: USDA measured 0 g; no galactose source in corn+oil+salt. Absent.

remove.name: Maltose C12H22O11
remove.reason: USDA measured 0 g free maltose in the finished chip (starch not hydrolyzed). Absent.

remove.name: Hexanoate (n-C6:0)
remove.reason: C6:0 = 0.0 g (USDA). Short/medium-chain FA of coconut/palm-kernel/dairy fat, not corn frying oil. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Octanoic acid
remove.reason: C8:0 = 0.01 g trace (USDA); coconut/palm-kernel marker, not a real corn-oil component. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Decanoic acid
remove.reason: C10:0 = 0.004 g trace (USDA); MCFA not characteristic of corn oil. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Dodecanoic acid
remove.reason: C12:0 (lauric) = 0.001 g trace (USDA); coconut/palm-kernel marker, absent in corn-oil chip. FooDB noise.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: Stereochemistry artifact; biological serine in corn protein is L-serine (kept as L-serine).

remove.name: Cyanocobalamin
remove.reason: Vitamin B12 = 0 in a plant food (USDA). No real cobalamin content.

remove.name: Vitamin D
remove.reason: Vitamin D = 0 (USDA); not a nutrient of an unfortified corn snack.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed retinol = 0 in a plant food (USDA); provitamin-A carotenoids only.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Free cobalt ion; non-nutritive trace, B12 = 0 so no functional cobalamin pool. Not a growth nutrient of this food.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a corn-chip growth nutrient.

missing_added: starch quantified as the DOMINANT carbon source (~51.4 g/100g)
organic acids: phytic (~0.7 g) plus small citric/malic/acetic/succinic/formic/lactic from peer-reviewed maize NMR data (FooDB had listed none)
L-phenylalanine and L-valine (both missing from the current FooDB amino-acid list); full measured amino-acid profile
corrected L-serine (FooDB record had non-biological D-serine)
quantified individual fatty acids (linoleic-dominant corn oil) and long-chain saturates
quantified full mineral panel (Na, Cl, K, P, Ca, Mg, Fe, Zn, Cu, Mn, Se, F)
quantified full vitamin panel; vitamin E corrected to 7.23 mg + gamma-tocopherol 17.8 mg; beta-cryptoxanthin added
genuine phytosterols with measured amounts (see keep_uncovered)
keep_uncovered: beta-sitosterol (148 mg/100g, measured USDA FDC 167537; genuine corn-oil phytosterol, no BiGG metabolite)
campesterol (51 mg/100g, measured; genuine corn-oil phytosterol)
stigmasterol (21 mg/100g, measured; genuine corn-oil phytosterol)
lutein + zeaxanthin (527 µg/100g, measured; genuine yellow-corn xanthophylls)
2',3'-dihydro-phytomenadione / dihydrophylloquinone (16.8 µg/100g, measured; vitamin-K vitamer from hydrogenated frying oil)
sources: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, 'Snacks, corn-based, extruded, chips, plain', FDC ID 167537 - full measured nutrient panel incl. amino acids, individual sugars, fatty acids, phytosterols, vitamins, minerals (primary source for all quantified values)
USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, 'Snacks, corn-based, extruded, chips, unsalted', FDC ID 168127 - cross-check of the same matrix
Nardi et al. 2024, Molecules 29:4097, doi:10.3390/molecules29174097 (PMC11397060) - quantitative maize-grain organic acids and free sugars (NMR metabolomics)
Urizar & Bressani 1997 phytate-in-nixtamal data, reviewed in Felix-Samano et al., Int J Food Sci 2025, doi:10.1155/ijfo/6961009 - phytic-acid estimate
notes: DOMINANT carbon/energy source is STARCH (~51 g/100g); free sugars are trivial (sucrose 0.27 g; glucose/fructose/maltose/lactose/galactose all 0). Because the food is UNFERMENTED, the classic organic acids are only trace - the one substantial acid is phytate (~0.7 g/100g), which also binds most of the phosphorus/mineral pool; small citric/malic/acetic/succinic/lactic pools were added from peer-reviewed maize data (FooDB listed no organic acids). Protein (6.17 g/100g) is maize zein/glutelin (glutamate-, leucine-, proline-rich; lysine/tryptophan-limiting); amino acids here are USDA-measured. Lipid (33.4 g/100g) is corn frying oil dominated by linoleic acid, with genuine measured phytosterols. Calcium is elevated by lime nixtamalization. Removed animal/dairy artifacts (cholesterol, lactose, galactose, retinol, B12, vitamin D), zero/near-zero MCFAs (C6-C12), the D-serine stereo-artifact, and non-nutritive trace ions (Co2+, Ni).
